front: Work: in this novel, an overweight Dominican sci-fi nerd at Rutgers is haunted by a fuku curse from the Trujillo regime; narrated by Yunior de las Casas
back: The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao (Junot Diaz)
work: The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao

front: Work: in this novel, both the protagonist and his mother Beli are beaten in canefields and rescued by a golden mongoose
back: The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao (Junot Diaz)
work: The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao

front: Work: in this novel, Beli sees a faceless man and a goat carcass while being driven from an outing with 'the Gangster'
back: The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao (Junot Diaz)
work: The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao

front: Work: in this novel, the narrator Yunior compares the dictator Rafael Trujillo to Sauron

front: Work: in this story, the narrator's ex-fiancee sends him emails of fifty women he cheated on her with; it ends with 'the half-life of love is forever'
back: "The Cheater's Guide to Love" (Junot Diaz, from This is How You Lose Her)
work: This is How You Lose Her

front: Work: this story gives dating advice in second person and ends with 'put the government cheese back in its place before your mom kills you'
back: "How to Date a Brown Girl" (Junot Diaz)
work: This is How You Lose Her

front: Work: in this story, a teenager has an affair with a muscular female teacher while discussing the nuclear apocalypse film 'The Day After'
back: "Miss Lora" (Junot Diaz, from This is How You Lose Her)
work: This is How You Lose Her

front: Work: this first collection by a Dominican-American author opens with 'Ysrael,' about a boy whose face was eaten by a pig
Author: this author wrote the essay 'MFA vs. POC' critiquing white-dominated creative writing programs; in 2018 Zinzi Clemmons accused him of harassment
Work: in this novel, Socorro jumps in front of a train, leading daughter Belicia to be raised by La Inca
back: The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao (Junot Diaz)
